title: Global Aid Reaches Venezuela After Deadly Earthquakes
subtitle: International teams join local volunteers as the death toll rises following twin quakes

summary.en: Venezuela is facing a major humanitarian crisis after two powerful earthquakes struck its northern coast. The official death toll has climbed to 920, with thousands more reported injured. Governments around the world have launched relief missions to provide medical support and search for survivors. Local residents and volunteers have been working tirelessly, often using their bare hands to dig through the rubble. Experts are now watching to see if this international support will be enough to stabilize the country in the long term.
summary.zh: 委內瑞拉北部海岸遭受兩起強震襲擊，正面臨嚴重的人道危機。官方統計死亡人數已攀升至九百二十人，另有數千人受傷。世界各國已啟動救援任務，提供醫療支援並搜尋倖存者。當地居民與志工正不懈地努力，常需徒手挖掘瓦礫。專家正密切關注這些國際支援是否足以讓該國長期穩定下來。

paragraphs.en: International rescue teams are arriving in Venezuela to help with the recovery after two massive earthquakes hit the country’s northern coast. The twin quakes, which measured 7.2 and 7.5 in magnitude, have caused widespread destruction. According to The Guardian, the official death toll has reached 920 people, while nearly 3,000 others have been injured. Interim President Delcy Rodríguez has promised that the government will do everything possible to save lives, but many families are still waiting for help in the most damaged areas.
paragraphs.zh: 國際救援隊正陸續抵達委內瑞拉，協助該國北部海岸遭受兩起強震後的復原工作。這兩起規模分別為七點二與七點五的地震造成了廣泛的破壞。根據《衛報》報導，官方統計死亡人數已達九百二十人，另有近三千人受傷。代理總統羅德里格斯（Delcy Rodríguez）承諾政府將竭盡所能拯救生命，但在受災最嚴重的地區，許多家庭仍在等待救援。

paragraphs.en: In the seaside region of La Guaira, the situation remains desperate. Many residents have expressed anger and frustration at the slow pace of the official government response. Paramedic Rotny Bombart, who spent hours searching for his mother in a collapsed building, told The Guardian that he saw no government emergency workers when he first arrived at the scene. In the absence of professional help, local volunteers have been digging through the wreckage with their bare hands and basic tools. Another local resident, Diego González, noted that the destruction in towns like Catia La Mar is so severe that very few buildings have survived the shaking.
paragraphs.zh: 在拉瓜伊拉（La Guaira）沿海地區，情況依然危急。許多居民對政府緩慢的應變速度感到憤怒與挫折。救護人員邦巴特（Rotny Bombart）在倒塌建築中搜尋母親數小時後，向《衛報》表示，他剛抵達現場時並未見到任何政府救難人員。在缺乏專業協助的情況下，當地志工只能徒手或使用簡易工具挖掘瓦礫。另一位當地居民岡薩雷斯（Diego González）指出，像卡蒂亞拉馬（Catia La Mar）這類城鎮的毀損極為嚴重，幾乎沒有建築物能在強震中倖存。

paragraphs.en: In response to the disaster, countries from around the world have sent aid. The United States has announced a major relief package, promising $150 million to support faith-based groups and United Nations agencies. According to NPR, the U.S. is also deploying search and rescue teams from Virginia and California, along with military support to help move supplies into the country. This effort marks a significant change in how the U.S. handles international disasters, following criticism of its response to a previous earthquake in Myanmar last year.
paragraphs.zh: 針對這場災難，世界各國紛紛伸出援手。美國宣布了一項大型援助計畫，承諾提供一億五千萬美元，以支持宗教團體與聯合國機構。根據美國國家公共廣播電台（NPR）報導，美國也正調派來自維吉尼亞州與加州的搜救隊，並提供軍事支援以協助運送物資進入該國。這項行動標誌著美國處理國際災難方式的重大轉變，此前美國曾因對去年緬甸地震的反應而飽受批評。

paragraphs.en: Other nations are also stepping up to assist. India has launched "Operation Amistad," which is the Spanish word for friendship. According to DW, the Indian government has sent two Air Force planes carrying over 35 tons of medical supplies and equipment. A 41-member medical unit from the Indian Army has also been deployed to provide urgent care. Furthermore, the United Kingdom has sent a 68-person search and rescue team, along with six specially trained dogs and £2 million in humanitarian funding. Many other countries, including Brazil, France, Spain, and Turkey, have also offered to send help.
paragraphs.zh: 其他國家也正加緊協助。印度發起了名為「友誼行動」（Operation Amistad）的救援計畫，此名稱源自西班牙語中的友誼一詞。根據德國之聲（DW）報導，印度政府已派遣兩架空軍運輸機，載運超過三十五噸的醫療物資與設備。一支由印度陸軍組成的四十一員醫療小組也已部署，以提供緊急醫療照護。此外，英國派遣了一支六十八人的搜救隊，隨行還有六隻受過專業訓練的搜救犬，並提供兩百萬英鎊的人道援助資金。包括巴西、法國、西班牙與土耳其在內的多個國家，也都主動提出援助。

paragraphs.en: Despite this wave of international support, experts warn that the road to recovery will be long. Jeremy Konyndyk, president of Refugees International, told NPR that while the immediate rescue phase is critical, the long-term success of the mission will depend on how well the international community helps restore basic services. He explained that stabilizing the health system and repairing damaged water infrastructure will be essential in the coming months. He noted that the world will be watching to see if the current administration can maintain its commitment to Venezuela after the initial rescue operations are finished.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管國際支援湧入，專家仍警告復原之路將會漫長。國際難民組織（Refugees International）主席柯寧迪克（Jeremy Konyndyk）向 NPR 表示，雖然緊急救援階段至關重要，但任務的長期成效將取決於國際社會協助恢復基礎服務的程度。他解釋說，在未來幾個月內，穩定醫療體系與修復受損的水利基礎設施將是關鍵。他並指出，全球將密切關注現任政府在初期救援行動結束後，能否維持對委內瑞拉的承諾。

paragraphs.en: For now, the focus remains on finding survivors and providing medical care to those who have lost their homes. The scale of the disaster has highlighted deep problems within the country, as years of economic issues and corruption have made it difficult for local authorities to respond effectively. As international teams begin to work alongside local volunteers, there is hope that the combined effort will save more lives. However, for the people of La Guaira and other affected areas, the memory of the destruction will last for a long time. The international community has made a strong start, but the true test of this relief effort will be its ability to provide lasting support to a country that has been deeply shaken by this tragedy.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，重點仍放在搜尋倖存者並為失去家園的災民提供醫療照護。這場災難的規模凸顯了該國深層的問題，多年來的經濟困境與貪腐，使得當地政府難以有效應對。隨著國際團隊開始與當地志工並肩作戰，人們期盼這股合力能拯救更多生命。然而，對於拉瓜伊拉及其他受災地區的民眾而言，這場毀滅性的記憶將長久揮之不去。國際社會已有了強而有力的開端，但這項救援行動真正的考驗，在於能否為這個因悲劇而深受重創的國家提供長久的支援。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase an effort or take action to help in a situation.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 加強行動、挺身而出（提供協助）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「走上台階」，這裡指採取行動。文中：Other nations are also stepping up to assist.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Deeply affected or upset by a shocking or traumatic event.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 受到嚴重衝擊的、心靈受創的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「搖晃」的過去分詞，這裡形容國家受災後的心理或社會狀態。文中：the true test of this relief effort will be its ability to provide lasting support to a country that has been deeply shaken by this tragedy.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To indicate or represent a significant point or change.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 標誌著、象徵（某個轉折點）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（分數、痕跡），這裡作動詞表示「象徵」。文中：This effort marks a significant change in how the U.S. handles international disasters, following criticism of its response to a previous earthquake in Myanmar last year.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 920 as interim president vows to save ‘as many as possible’

sources.name: NPR News
sources.title: U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: India news: Government sends help to quake-hit Venezuela

previousArticles.title: Venezuela Faces Humanitarian Crisis After Powerful Double Earthquakes
previousArticles.summaryEn: Venezuela is currently dealing with the aftermath of two major earthquakes that struck the country's northern coast on Wednesday. At least 188 people have been confirmed dead, with over 1,500 others reported injured. The government has declared a state of emergency as rescue crews continue to search for survivors. International leaders have pledged significant financial and practical support to assist with the recovery efforts. The United Nations is coordinating a large-scale humanitarian response to help millions of people already in need.

previousArticles.title: International Operation Begins to Rescue Sailors Trapped in Strait of Hormuz
previousArticles.summaryEn: The United Nations has launched a major operation to evacuate over 11,000 sailors trapped in the Strait of Hormuz. This effort follows a recent peace agreement between the United States and Iran to end their ongoing conflict. While shipping traffic has begun to increase, significant disagreements remain between the two nations. US officials continue to reject Iranian plans to charge tolls for using the international waterway. Meanwhile, regional stability remains fragile as diplomatic talks continue in several countries.

quiz.question: What specific actions are the U.S. and India taking to assist in the Venezuelan earthquake recovery?

options.A: Both countries are primarily focusing on providing direct financial grants to the Venezuelan government.
options.B: The U.S. is sending search teams and military logistics, while India is providing medical personnel and supplies.
options.C: The U.S. is sending medical units to La Guaira, while India is focusing on building new infrastructure.
options.D: Both countries are sending only canine search units to help locate survivors under the rubble.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，美國部署了來自維吉尼亞州和加州的搜救隊及軍事支援；第四段則提到印度派遣了 41 人的醫療小組並運送了 35 噸醫療物資。選項 A 錯誤，因為美國資助的是宗教團體與聯合國機構，而非直接給委內瑞拉政府。選項 C 混淆了兩國的任務內容。選項 D 錯誤，因為只有英國提到了派遣搜救犬，這不是美印兩國的主要任務。

quiz.question: Based on the accounts of local residents and the observations of experts, what is a likely reason for the slow initial response to the earthquake?

options.A: The international community was hesitant to send aid due to the high magnitude of the quakes.
options.B: The government had already successfully restored all basic services before international teams arrived.
options.C: Existing systemic issues, such as corruption and economic instability, hindered the local government's ability to act.
options.D: Local volunteers refused to work with official government emergency responders during the first few hours.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第六段指出，該國長期的經濟問題與腐敗使得當地政府難以有效應對災害，這解釋了第二段中居民對政府反應緩慢的憤怒。選項 A 與文中提到的國際社會積極援助相矛盾。選項 B 明顯錯誤，文中提到基礎設施受損嚴重且政府反應遲緩。選項 D 並非事實，文中僅提到在缺乏專業協助時，志工不得不親自挖掘，並未提到拒絕與政府合作。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by the phrase 'a country that has been deeply shaken by this tragedy'?

options.A: The country is physically unstable because the ground continues to experience aftershocks.
options.B: The citizens are experiencing profound emotional and societal trauma beyond just physical damage.
options.C: The government's political structure has been physically dismantled by the force of the earthquake.
options.D: The country's economy is being rebuilt using modern, earthquake-resistant construction methods.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。這裡的「shaken」一詞採用雙關語，既指物理上的地震震動，也隱喻國家因災難遭受了巨大的精神與社會衝擊。選項 A 過於狹隘，僅指物理層面，忽略了「tragedy」一詞帶來的社會意涵。選項 C 將比喻過度字面化，文中並未提到政府結構被物理摧毀。選項 D 與文章內容無關，文中並未討論建築技術的更新。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The earthquake in Venezuela has successfully united the government and local volunteers in a perfect rescue operation.
options.B: While international aid is providing a necessary start, the long-term recovery of Venezuela remains uncertain due to internal challenges.
options.C: The United States and India are competing to see which nation can provide the most effective humanitarian relief to Venezuela.
options.D: The primary cause of the high death toll in Venezuela was the lack of international search and rescue teams on the ground.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章從災難發生、國際援助到專家對於長期恢復的擔憂進行了全面報導，特別強調了第五、六段中提到的長期挑戰與內部問題。選項 A 過於樂觀，忽略了文中提到的政府反應緩慢與內部腐敗。選項 C 錯誤，文中並未提到兩國存在競爭關係。選項 D 將死亡人數歸咎於缺乏國際援助，但文中指出是因為當地政府應對能力不足與災害規模過大所致。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
